title: Asia, Europe Stocks Widen Lead Over US Benchmarks: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks in Asia and Europe were poised to outperform US benchmarks in February, as the so-called AI scare trade that rattled Wall Street prompted investors to rotate into markets seen as more insulated from disruption risks.

title: Gold Inches Higher as US and Iran Agree to Prolong Nuclear Talks
summary: Gold rose marginally after the US and Iran agreed to further nuclear talks, with an American military build-up in the Middle East keeping markets on edge about the potential for conflict.

title: Fairfax Said to Lead Race for $8 Billion India Bank Stake Sale
summary: Fairfax Financial Holdings Ltd. is the frontrunner to buy a majority stake in IDBI Bank Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter, in what could be the biggest foreign investment in India’s banking sector.

title: Son of ‘Gasoline Godfather’ Jailed in Graft Case
summary: The son of a reclusive oil merchant who dominated Indonesia’s fuel trade for decades received a prison sentence as part of a $17 billion corruption case centered on the country’s state-owned energy firm.

title: HK Government to Inject New Capital Into Investment Fund

title: IDC Sees Smartphone Market Crash on Chip Crunch | Bloomberg Tech: Asia 2/27/2026
summary: The unintended consequences of the memory chip shortage are escalating. title: Korea’s $1 Trillion Pension Fund Has Record Gains on Kospi Rally
summary: South Korea’s National Pension Service, one of the world’s largest public pension funds, posted its strongest-ever annual return in 2025 as a semiconductor- and artificial intelligence–driven stock rally boosted performance.

title: Hong Kong’s Shock Move to Tap Dollar Defense Fund Fuels Concern
summary: Hong Kong’s proposal to pull HK$150 billion ($19 billion) from a fund used to defend the currency’s peg with the greenback shocked some officials and raised concern over the precedent it sets.

title: Japan 2-Year Bond Sale Passes Without Drama Amid BOJ Speculation
summary: Japan’s two-year government bond auction passed through smoothly as investors mull the Bank of Japan’s rate-hike path.

title: Oil Steadies With More Nuclear Talks Planned as US Forces Gather
summary: Oil steadied after the US and Iran agreed to more nuclear talks next week following a round of discussions on Thursday, as a huge deployment of American forces in the Middle East kept the market on edge.
